bandla-v-solicitors-regulation-authority.lesson: Former solicitors filing pro se still face indemnity costs when fake authorities are maintained after challenge.

crypto-open-patent-alliance-v-wright-2-2.lesson: Pro-se AI filings can trigger GCROs and AG referrals when fabricated authorities accompany a pattern of abuse.

reclamação-rcl-78890-ba-2025-05-12.lesson: AI watermarks on filings are red flags—review every output before submission or face OAB referral and doubled costs.

case-no-72079-11-24-2025-05-12.lesson: Separating personal AI drafts from court filings requires a verification checkpoint—one wrong-file error triggers bar referral.

jamisson-roriz-de-santana-andrade-v-trib.lesson: MobiOffice and similar built-in AI assistants require the same verification discipline as ChatGPT—courts treat watermarks as admission.

peter-gilmai-v-abel-tol-ors-2025-05-09.lesson: ChatGPT citations must be verified against PacLII or equivalent before filing—admission triggers Law Society referral.

israel-v-ibrahim-mahajneh-2025-05-07.lesson: Prosecutors must verify statutes exist before citing—fictional laws damage institutional credibility even without formal sanction.

lozano-gonzález-v-roberge-2025-05-01.lesson: AI translations of foreign-language statutes are unreliable—always engage qualified counsel for cross-border civil code interpretation.

nexgen-pathology-services-ltd-v-darcueil.lesson: Blaming a junior researcher does not shield you—supervising counsel is liable for unverified citations attributed to anyone.

beschluss-5-u-125-2025-04-29.lesson: German courts will silently discard Fehlzitate—unverified citations waste advocacy even without formal sanction.

anonymous-v-anonymous-2025-04-29.lesson: Misnumbering is not a credible defense when citations do not exist anywhere—personal costs follow denial.

simpson-v-hung-long-enterprises-inc-2025.lesson: Pro-se AI submissions still trigger costs when opposing parties waste time chasing fake authorities.

goshen-multiservice-limited-v-accuro-env.lesson: Tribunals flag AI-generated authorities even without sanction—unverifiable citations erode credibility entirely.

rowe-v-national-australia-bank-ltd-2025-.lesson: Fabricated High Court authorities in pro-se filings are detected quickly—no tactical advantage, only wasted credibility.

vilmar-martins-dos-santos-v-state-of-par.lesson: Defense counsel must cross-check relator names and process numbers—implausible patterns expose AI fabrication fast.

shekartz-v-assuta-ashdod-ltd-2025-04-07.lesson: Israeli courts are now imposing monetary sanctions on AI-related filing errors without requiring detailed proof of bad faith.

zzaman-v-hmrc-2025-04-03.lesson: Even accurate case names can be misapplied by AI—verify that cited authority actually supports your proposition.

ayinde-v-borough-of-haringey-2025-04-03.lesson: Both barrister and solicitor face wasted costs when fake cases slip through—dual-review is non-negotiable.

bangholme-investments-pty-ltd-v-greater-.lesson: AI search summaries of procedural rules are routinely wrong—read the statute directly before relying on AI inference.

mazurek-et-al-v-thomazoni-2025-04-02.lesson: ChatGPT-generated TJPR citations with retired judges are easily detected—verify relator chamber and tenure before filing.
